title: docs(kube play): annotation flag is missleading
body:
## Description

While experimenting with https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/25185 I noticed that the `--annotation` flag used for `kube play` has no effect to the pods created.

Here is the documentation for the flag

https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/d680c48eafba45205608bdeaadd0cc5e90a675a2/cmd/podman/kube/play.go#L107

However, the pod interface **do not have** an annotations property. Only `Labels`

https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/55b6e4c3e8bf10994a419cb314eaa296670d1d7d/libpod/pod.go#L48

I would suggest to change it to `Add annotations to containers in the created pods (key=value)` or something similar, to better express, that only containers will have the annotation.

> ⚠ The infra container is not receiving the annotation, is it expected ? it is a bug?

---

I am willing to open the PR, you can assign me

comments.body: Is `--annotation` meant to set annotations on the containers, instead of labels on the pod?

FWIW, annotations at the container level don't exist in Kubernetes, so for consistency (and to support generating the corresponding k8s yaml), podman should add annotations to the Pod, not to the containers.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
